svg.text: SANSKRIT
HISTORY & THE MITHILA CONNECTION
SANSKRIT — LANGUAGE OF THE VEDAS
~1500 BCE: Vedic Sanskrit — oldest attested Indo-Iranian
Rigveda: oldest religious text still in use worldwide
~500-400 BCE: Classical Sanskrit codified by Pāṇini (Ashtadhyayi)
Pānini’s grammar: 3,959 sutras — world’s first formal grammar
Sanskrit = "refined" (sam + krita = "put together perfectly")
CLASSICAL SANSKRIT LITERATURE
Mahabharata (400 BCE-400 CE): 1.8M words — longest epic ever
Ramayana: Valmiki’s 24,000 verses — Rama’s exile and return
Kalidasa (4th c. CE): Shakuntala, Meghaduta — Sanskrit’s height
Ayurveda, Arthashastra, Natyashastra: sciences in Sanskrit
SANSKRIT INFLUENCE ON WORLD LANGUAGES
Proto-Indo-European ancestor: Sanskrit shares roots with Greek, Latin
Sanskrit asvah = Latin equus = English "equine" (horse)
Sanskrit matar = Latin mater = English "mother"
Influenced Persian, Arabic (via loanwords), SE Asian languages
MAITHILI HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
MITHILA — LAND OF JANAKA AND SITA
Mithila: ancient kingdom mentioned in the Ramayana and Mahabharata
King Janaka of Mithila: father of Sita (Ram’s wife)
Janaka’s court: famous Sanskrit philosophical debates (Upanishads)
Yaajnavalkya and Gargi debated there — first female philosopher?
MAITHILI LITERARY TRADITION
Vidyapati (c. 1352-1448): greatest Maithili poet
Padavali: Sanskrit-Maithili devotional songs for Radha-Krishna
Vidyapati’s language: bridge between Sanskrit and vernacular
Songs still sung at weddings and festivals in Bihar and Nepal
MAITHILI RECOGNITION
1965: Sahitya Akademi recognizes Maithili as Indian literature
2003: Added to Eighth Schedule of Indian Constitution
~34M speakers in Bihar (India) and Madhesh Province (Nepal)
Maithili in Nepal: second most spoken language after Nepali
THE SANSKRIT-MAITHILI THREAD
Mithila scribes: wrote both Sanskrit texts and Maithili poetry
Tirhuta script: used for Sanskrit and Maithili interchangeably
3000 years of Sanskrit scholarship flowing into Maithili tongue
PLATE 15d ALT · SANSKRIT / MAITHILI · HISTORY
